name: High-Stakes Poker at The Bellagio
description: The most iconic poker room in Las Vegas. Minimum buy-in $500. The felt is green, the stakes are real, and the legends of the game have all sat at these tables.

name: Roulette at The Wynn
description: European roulette with single zero. The Wynn's tables are plush, the dealers are sharp, and the cocktail waitresses never let your glass go empty.

name: Blackjack Marathon at The Cosmopolitan
description: The Cosmo's blackjack tables have the best minimums on the Strip. Sit down, count your cards, and try not to lose your shirt before sunrise.

name: Craps at Caesars Palace
description: The most social game on the floor. When the dice are hot, the whole table screams. When they're cold, everyone goes quiet. Pure theatre.

name: Slot Tournament at The Venetian
description: Pull levers alongside 200 strangers all chasing the same jackpot. Low stakes, high energy, maximum chaos.

name: VIP Table at Hakkasan Nightclub
description: Five floors of bass, bottle service, and bodies. World-class DJs spin until 4 AM. The VIP section has the best sightlines in Vegas.

name: Cocktails at The Chandelier Bar
description: Three levels of crystalline luxury inside The Cosmopolitan. Order the verbena cocktail on level 1.5 — it changes flavor as you drink it.

name: Rooftop Party at Drai's
description: Open-air nightclub on top of The Cromwell. The Strip glitters below while hip-hop royalty rules the decks above.

name: Underground Jazz at The Barbershop
description: Hidden behind a barbershop facade in The Cosmo. Live jazz, craft whiskey, and the kind of ambiance Sinatra would have approved of.

name: Neon Nightclub Crawl
description: Three clubs, no lines, all VIP. Start at XS, move to Omnia, end at Marquee. Your feet will hate you. Your memories won't exist.

name: Dinner at Hell's Kitchen
description: Gordon Ramsay's Vegas outpost at Caesars Palace. The beef Wellington is non-negotiable. The sticky toffee pudding is the afterlife.

name: Omakase at Nobu
description: Thirteen courses of chef's choice sushi at the Nobu at Caesars. Each piece is a sculpture. Each bite is a revelation.

name: Buffet at The Wynn
description: The most lavish buffet in Vegas. Crab legs, prime rib, and sixteen desserts that would make a pastry chef cry.

name: Steakhouse at SW at The Wynn
description: Japanese A5 Wagyu, dry-aged tomahawk, truffle mac. The wine list is longer than some novels. Dress code enforced.

name: Late Night Taco Crawl
description: Follow the locals to the hidden taco trucks that only appear after midnight. Al pastor, birria, and carnitas that taste like religion.

name: Cirque du Soleil — O
description: Water, acrobatics, and a dream sequence you won't forget. The pinnacle of Vegas spectacle at the Bellagio.

name: Cirque du Soleil — Mystère
description: The original Cirque Vegas show at Treasure Island. Jaw-dropping acrobatics, surreal clowning, and a live score that shakes your chest.

name: Penn & Teller Live
description: The greatest magic-comedy duo alive. They'll show you how the trick works and you still won't believe it.

name: Electric Burlesque Show
description: The most daring cabaret show on the Strip. Sequins, seduction, and spectacle. Not for the faint-hearted.

name: Comedy Cellar at Rio
description: Uncensored stand-up from comics who have nothing left to lose. Two-drink minimum. Infinite laughs.

name: Pool Party at Encore Beach Club
description: Sun, bass, and champagne. DJs start at noon and the party doesn't stop until sunset. Cabana upgrade strongly recommended.

name: Wet Republic Ultra Pool
description: MGM Grand's legendary pool party. Two saltwater pools, world-famous DJs, and the kind of people-watching you can't get anywhere else.

name: Daylight Beach Club
description: Mandalay Bay's sun-soaked paradise. Lazy river, wave pool, and a DJ booth that faces the sunset.

name: Forum Shops at Caesars
description: Roman-themed mall with luxury brands. Gucci, Versace, Louis Vuitton — all under a painted sky ceiling that shifts from dawn to dusk.

name: Miracle Mile Shops
description: 200 stores in a mile-long loop at Planet Hollywood. More affordable than the Forum, but still dangerously tempting.

name: Fremont Street Experience
description: The world's largest LED screen canopy comes alive every hour. Five blocks of old-school Vegas neon, street performers, and chaos. Free admission.

name: Neon Museum Tour
description: Where old Vegas signs go to rest. A graveyard of glowing history. The night tour is magical — the signs light up against the desert sky.

name: Hidden Speakeasy Tour
description: Three bars. Three passwords. One guide who knows every secret door in Vegas. Craft cocktails in rooms most tourists never find.

name: Bellagio Fountain Show
description: 1,000 fountains dancing to music every 15 minutes. Free, iconic, and somehow still breathtaking the tenth time you see it.

name: High Roller Observation Wheel
description: 550 feet above the Strip in a glass pod. The Happy Hour cabin comes with an open bar. Best views in Vegas, no gambling required.

name: Indoor Skydiving at Vegas Indoor Skydiving
description: Float on a column of air without jumping out of a plane. Perfect for adrenaline junkies who like their risks calculated.

name: Pawn Stars — Gold & Silver Pawn Shop
description: The actual shop from the TV show. Browse vintage watches, rare coins, and the kind of stuff only Vegas produces. Free entry.

name: Desert Highway Sunset Drive
description: Rent a convertible and hit the open road toward Red Rock Canyon. The desert sunset paints the sky in colours that Instagram can't capture.

name: Topgolf Las Vegas
description: Four floors of golf bays overlooking the Strip. Cocktails, competition, and climate-controlled comfort. You don't need to be good at golf.

name: Gondola Ride at The Venetian
description: A singing gondolier, a fake Italian canal, and absolutely zero irony. This is Vegas distilled — take it or leave it.

name: Baccarat at The Aria
description: The game of kings. High limits, low noise, and the kind of tension that makes your palms sweat. Minimum bet $100.

name: Sports Betting at Circa
description: The world's largest sportsbook. A three-story screen, 1,000 seats, and the roar of a crowd that lives and dies by every play.

name: Absinthe Show
description: Performed in a tiny tent outside Caesars. Acrobatics, comedy, and content so R-rated it'll make you blush. The best show in Vegas.

name: Area 15 Immersive Experience
description: Part art installation, part nightclub, part fever dream. Meow Wolf's Omega Mart is inside. You will question reality.

name: Spa Day at The Venetian
description: Hot stone massage, eucalyptus steam room, and a plunge pool that resets your entire nervous system. The hangover cure you deserve.

name: Helicopter Night Tour
description: See the Strip from 600 feet in the air. The city becomes a river of light. Some tours include champagne. All include awe.

name: Lip Smacking Foodie Tour
description: A guided walking tour through the best off-Strip restaurants. Five stops, ten dishes, and stories about the chefs who make them.

name: Blue Man Group
description: Three bald blue men create percussive chaos with PVC pipes, paint, and audience participation. Family-friendly but still fun after drinks.

name: Luxury Shopping at The Wynn Esplanade
description: Hermès, Chanel, Dior, and Rolex — all under one gilded roof. The kind of shopping where they bring you champagne while you browse.

name: Mob Museum
description: The real history of organised crime in Vegas. Interactive exhibits, a courtroom where mobsters were tried, and a speakeasy in the basement.
